title: Europe Faces Dangerous Heatwave as Temperatures Climb
subtitle: Authorities across the continent implement emergency measures as record-breaking heat continues to impact millions.

summary.en: A severe heatwave is currently affecting large parts of Europe, leading to record-breaking temperatures and emergency red alerts. France has taken strict measures, including banning public alcohol consumption and closing hundreds of schools to protect citizens. Meteorologists explain that a mass of hot air from the Sahara is trapped over the region, creating a dangerous 'heat dome.' Transportation services have faced significant disruptions, with many trains cancelled due to the extreme conditions. Health officials continue to urge extreme caution as the heat is expected to last for several more days.
summary.zh: 一場嚴重的熱浪目前正影響歐洲大部分地區，導致氣溫破紀錄並發布緊急紅色警報。法國已採取嚴格措施，包括禁止公共場所飲酒及關閉數百所學校以保護公民。氣象學家解釋，一團來自撒哈拉沙漠的熱空氣滯留在該地區上空，形成了危險的「熱穹」。交通運輸面臨嚴重中斷，許多列車因極端氣候而取消。衛生官員持續呼籲民眾保持高度警覺，因為預計高溫還將持續數日。

paragraphs.en: A dangerous heatwave is currently gripping much of Europe, forcing governments to issue emergency alerts and cancel public events. As temperatures climb toward record levels, authorities are working to protect the public from what experts describe as an exceptionally severe and long-lasting weather event. The heat is being driven by a mass of hot air moving north from the Sahara desert, which has become trapped over Western and Central Europe. Meteorologists refer to this high-pressure system as a "heat dome," which prevents the air from moving and allows temperatures to rise day after day.
paragraphs.zh: 一場危險的熱浪目前正席捲歐洲大部分地區，迫使各國政府發布緊急警報並取消公共活動。隨著氣溫攀升至破紀錄的水準，當局正致力於保護民眾，免受專家所稱這場極度嚴重且持久的天氣事件影響。這波熱浪是由一團從撒哈拉沙漠向北移動的熱空氣所引起，該氣團滯留在西歐與中歐上空。氣象學家將這種高壓系統稱為「熱穹」，它阻礙了空氣流動，導致氣溫日復一日地持續升高。

paragraphs.en: France is at the center of the crisis, with roughly half of the country under a red heat alert—the highest level of warning. According to BBC News, temperatures in the south-western city of Bordeaux recently reached 40 degrees Celsius. The government has taken significant steps to manage the risk, including closing nearly 850 schools on Monday, while over 1,500 other schools have allowed students to leave early. The Guardian reported that Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu held emergency meetings to discuss how the country can better adapt to these frequent and intense heatwaves in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 法國是這場危機的中心，全國約有一半地區處於紅色高溫警報之下，這是最高等級的警告。根據英國廣播公司新聞網報導，西南部城市波爾多（Bordeaux）近期的氣溫已達到攝氏四十度。政府已採取重大措施來控制風險，包括在週一關閉近八百五十所學校，另有一千五百多所學校允許學生提早放學。衛報報導指出，總理塞巴斯蒂安·勒科爾尼（Sébastien Lecornu）召開了緊急會議，討論國家未來該如何更好地適應這些頻繁且強烈的熱浪。

paragraphs.en: Public life has been heavily affected by the heat. The annual Fête de la Musique, a popular nationwide music festival, took place under strict new rules. To keep people safe and reduce the pressure on emergency services, the government banned alcohol consumption in public places within areas under red alert. In Paris, authorities also restricted the sale of strong drinks along the banks of the Seine and the Canal St-Martin. Despite these challenges, the city has tried to provide relief by keeping parks and gardens open throughout the night and installing over 1,300 free public water fountains for residents and tourists.
paragraphs.zh: 大眾生活深受高溫影響。年度音樂節「音樂節」（Fête de la Musique）在嚴格的新規定下舉行。為了確保民眾安全並減輕緊急服務單位的壓力，政府禁止在紅色警報區域內的公共場所飲酒。在巴黎，當局也限制了塞納河與聖馬丁運河沿岸烈酒的販售。儘管面臨這些挑戰，該市仍試圖提供緩解措施，包括讓公園與花園徹夜開放，並為居民與遊客設置了一千三百多座免費公共飲水台。

paragraphs.en: Transportation networks are also struggling. Deutsche Welle reported that the extreme heat has caused concerns about the safety of rail infrastructure, as high temperatures can cause power lines to fail and tracks to expand. As a result, the French rail operator SNCF cancelled dozens of intercity trains. Jean Castex, the head of the state rail service, noted that thousands of staff members have been mobilized to monitor the network and conduct emergency repairs. Passengers have been strongly advised to delay any non-essential travel until the weather improves.
paragraphs.zh: 交通網路也陷入困境。德國之聲報導稱，極端高溫引發了對鐵路基礎設施安全性的擔憂，因為高溫可能導致電力線路故障與軌道膨脹。因此，法國國家鐵路公司（SNCF）取消了數十班城際列車。國家鐵路服務負責人讓·卡斯泰（Jean Castex）指出，已有數千名工作人員被動員起來監控網路並進行緊急維修。當局強烈建議乘客在天氣好轉前，延後所有非必要的行程。

paragraphs.en: Other parts of Europe are facing similar dangers. In Italy, red alerts have been issued for eight major cities, including Milan, Florence, and Turin. Meanwhile, Spain’s weather agency has warned that temperatures could hit 40 degrees across large parts of the country, leading to the cancellation of public events like sports screenings. In Germany, the Berlin Open tennis tournament was forced to suspend matches due to severe weather, and the German Life-Saving Association has issued urgent warnings against swimming in rivers and lakes, noting that several people have already gone missing or drowned while trying to cool off.
paragraphs.zh: 歐洲其他地區也面臨類似的危險。在義大利，包括米蘭、佛羅倫斯與杜林在內的八個主要城市已發布紅色警報。同時，西班牙氣象局警告，該國大片地區氣溫可能達到四十度，導致體育賽事轉播等公共活動被迫取消。在德國，柏林網球公開賽因惡劣天氣被迫暫停比賽，德國救生協會也發布緊急警告，呼籲民眾不要在河流與湖泊游泳，並指出已有數人在試圖消暑時失蹤或溺斃。

paragraphs.en: Scientists emphasize that these extreme heat events are becoming more frequent and intense as the Earth continues to warm. According to the World Health Organization, heatwaves are deadly, with over 200,000 people across the continent dying from heat-related causes in the last four years. The current situation is particularly concerning because it is the second heatwave to hit the region this year, and it is happening earlier than usual in the summer season.
paragraphs.zh: 科學家強調，隨著地球持續暖化，這些極端高溫事件正變得越來越頻繁且強烈。根據世界衛生組織的數據，熱浪致命性極高，過去四年內歐洲大陸有超過二十萬人死於與高溫相關的原因。目前的情況特別令人擔憂，因為這是今年襲擊該地區的第二波熱浪，且發生時間比往年夏季更早。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the situation remains uncertain. Météo-France stated that it is difficult to predict exactly how long this heatwave will last, though some forecasts suggest it could continue until the end of the week. Government officials have called for "extreme vigilance" and urged citizens to take all necessary precautions to stay hydrated and avoid direct sun. As the continent waits for temperatures to drop, the focus remains on protecting the most vulnerable members of society, including the elderly and those with health conditions, from the life-threatening impact of the heat.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，情況仍充滿不確定性。法國氣象局（Météo-France）表示，很難精確預測這波熱浪會持續多久，儘管部分預報顯示它可能會持續到本週末。政府官員呼籲民眾保持「極度警覺」，並敦促公民採取一切必要預防措施，以保持水分並避免直接曝曬於陽光下。在歐洲大陸等待氣溫下降的同時，重點仍放在保護社會中最脆弱的成員，包括老年人與患有健康疾病的民眾，免受高溫帶來的致命威脅。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To hold someone or something very tightly; in this context, to have a powerful and often negative effect on a place.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 緊抓、籠罩（在此處指負面影響籠罩某地）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「緊握」之意，這裡用來形容天氣對地區的強烈影響。文中：A dangerous heatwave is currently gripping much of Europe, forcing governments to issue emergency alerts and cancel public events.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be caused or forced by a particular thing.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「駕駛」車輛，這裡指天氣現象的成因。文中：The heat is being driven by a mass of hot air moving north from the Sahara desert, which has become trapped over Western and Central Europe.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become less hot or to make something less hot.
keyTerms.noteZh: 雖然字面意思容易理解，但作為慣用語，它專指透過某種行為（如游泳）來降低體溫。文中：the German Life-Saving Association has issued urgent warnings against swimming in rivers and lakes, noting that several people have already gone missing or drowned while trying to cool off.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Half of France under red heat alert as alcohol banned at street music festival

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: France cancels events and restricts alcohol consumption amid brutal heatwave

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Severe heat wave sweeps Europe, triggering alerts and disruptions

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Dangerous Heatwave as Temperatures Climb
previousArticles.summaryEn: A severe heatwave is hitting large parts of Europe, leading to record-breaking temperatures and emergency red alerts. France has taken strict measures, including banning public alcohol consumption and closing hundreds of schools. The extreme weather is caused by a high-pressure system bringing hot air from the Sahara desert. Other countries, including Italy, Spain, and Germany, are also dealing with dangerous conditions and public safety concerns. Experts warn that these intense heat events are becoming more frequent and longer due to global climate change.

quiz.question: What specific measure did French authorities implement to manage the public impact of the heatwave during the Fête de la Musique?

options.A: They canceled the music festival entirely across the country.
options.B: They prohibited the consumption of alcohol in public areas under red alerts.
options.C: They closed all parks and gardens in Paris to prevent crowds.
options.D: They required all tourists to stay indoors during the night.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，政府為了減少緊急服務的壓力，在紅色警戒區的公共場所禁止飲酒。選項 A 錯誤，因為節日仍有舉行，只是實施了嚴格的新規定。選項 C 與文章敘述相反，文章提到巴黎為了提供避暑空間，反而讓公園與花園徹夜開放。選項 D 並未在文中提及，屬於無中生有的選項。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the current heatwave be considered more dangerous than a typical summer heat event?

options.A: Because it is the first time the Sahara desert has affected European weather.
options.B: Because it is occurring earlier in the season and is the second one this year.
options.C: Because the heat dome is moving rapidly across the continent.
options.D: Because it caused the cancellation of the Berlin Open tennis tournament.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第六段，科學家指出目前情況特別令人擔憂，因為這是今年內發生的第二次熱浪，且比夏季往常的時間點更早發生。選項 A 錯誤，文中並未說這是首次發生。選項 C 與第一段敘述矛盾，熱穹（heat dome）的特性正是阻止空氣流動，而非快速移動。選項 D 雖然是事實，但這是一個單一事件，並非解釋為何「整個熱浪」比往常更危險的整體原因。

quiz.question: In the first paragraph, what does the word 'gripping' mean as used in the context of the heatwave?

options.A: Holding something tightly with the hands.
options.C: Affecting a place or person intensely or powerfully.
options.D: Traveling across a large distance quickly.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。在文中「A dangerous heatwave is currently gripping much of Europe」一句裡，gripping 用來形容熱浪對歐洲的強烈影響，意指「籠罩」或「緊緊控制」。選項 A 是其字面意思，但在氣候語境下不適用。選項 B 是指理解力，與氣候無關。選項 D 雖然熱浪移動，但 gripping 本身並非指移動的過程，而是指其造成的狀態。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main message of the article?

options.A: France is the only country in Europe struggling to manage the recent extreme weather.
options.B: The Sahara desert is solely responsible for the economic collapse of European rail services.
options.C: European nations are facing significant challenges due to increasingly frequent and severe heatwaves.
options.D: Public festivals are the primary cause of heat-related illnesses in Europe this summer.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文全面探討了熱浪對歐洲多國（如法國、義大利、西班牙、德國）的影響，包括公共安全、交通基礎設施以及政府的應對措施，並強調氣候變遷導致熱浪趨於頻繁。選項 A 錯誤，文章也提到了其他國家的災情。選項 B 過度誇大，且將熱浪歸咎於單一經濟後果。選項 D 錯誤，節慶只是受影響的活動之一，絕非熱浪致病的主因。
